description:
# Stretch fixture: tagged-tuple AST evaluator

## What

Add `fixtures/ast_eval/` — a multi-clause fn that pattern-matches
on tagged tuples `{:num, n}`, `{:add, a, b}`, `{:mul, a, b}` and
recursively evaluates to an int. Classic Erlang "eval" teaching
shape; first fixture to exercise tagged-tuple dispatch end-to-end
across all three execution paths.

```fz
fn eval({:num, n}), do: n
fn eval({:add, a, b}), do: eval(a) + eval(b)
fn eval({:mul, a, b}), do: eval(a) * eval(b)

fn main() do
  print(eval({:add, {:num, 2}, {:mul, {:num, 3}, {:num, 4}}}))
end
```

Expected stdout: `14\n` (2 + 3*4).

## Single-print only

The fixture uses **one** `print(eval(...))` rather than the more
demonstrative two-or-more sequence. Reason: fz-i82 (open bug)
truncates main's CPS chain after the first call when the called fn
takes tuple arguments. Multi-print form would silently miscompile.
When fz-i82 lands, a follow-up fixture can demonstrate the
multi-print stretch.

## Atomic deliverables

1. `fixtures/ast_eval/input.fz` — the program above
2. `fixtures/ast_eval/README.md` with frontmatter
   (`purpose: …`, `paths: [jit, interp, aot]`)
3. `expected.txt` blessed via `BLESS=1 cargo test fixture_matrix`
4. `expected.specs` blessed via `BLESS_SPECS=1 cargo test …`
5. `expected.clif` blessed via `BLESS=1 cargo test golden_clif`
6. `fixtures/index.md` regenerated via
   `BLESS=1 cargo test fixture_index_up_to_date`

## Try the repl leg

Attempt `paths: [jit, interp, aot, repl]`. If repl runs cleanly,
include it. If it fails, drop to `[jit, interp, aot]` and file a
separate ticket for the repl leg — do not include repl with a
defer rationale (per [[feedback_no_silent_deferrals]]).

## Blocked by

- fz-ul4.38 (AOT register tuple schemas) — without it the aot leg
  panics on first tuple alloc.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test fixture_matrix ast_eval` passes all declared paths.
- `cargo test fixture_index_up_to_date` passes.
- All existing fixture trials still pass.

## Out of scope

- Multi-print stretch (waits on fz-i82).
- Larger expression languages (variables, let, conditionals) —
  natural follow-ups once this lands and fz-i82 is resolved.
title: fixture: ast_eval — Erlang-style expression evaluator
